tool_args.label: LIRR & Metro-North Key Performance Metrics - February 2023

payload.title: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.description: Key performance metrics report prepared for the February 2023 meeting of the Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees

evidence_refs.source_quote: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
February 2023
This performance metrics document was prepared for the February 2023 meeting of the Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees.
February 21, 2023

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. Operates 700 trains daily and maintains 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Long Island Rail Road is comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.

payload.description: Comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. Operates 700 trains daily and maintains 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North Railroad is comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 150,000 passengers a day. We operate 700 trains daily and maintain 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

payload.description: North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.
MTA Construction & Development is building a unified trash facility for Metro-North and LIRR at Grand Central

payload.title: President, Metro-North Railroad Interim President, Long Island Rail Road

evidence_refs.source_quote: Cathy Rinaldi President, Metro-North Railroad Interim President, Long Island Rail Road

payload.status: opened January 25, 2023; full service beginning February 27, 2023
payload.description: New LIRR terminal at Grand Central, providing access to Manhattan's East Side. Full service adds 271 trains to weekday schedule, bringing total to 936 daily trains, with almost 300 operating to or from GCM.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Grand Central Madison Month One / Full Service On Deck

tool_args.label: Metro-North and LIRR Unified Trash Facility at Grand Central

payload.project_name: Metro-North and LIRR Unified Trash Facility at Grand Central
payload.status: construction contract substantially completed; working towards final completion in second quarter of 2023
payload.description: Unified trash facility for Metro-North and LIRR at Grand Central providing waste management for joint operation of Grand Central Terminal and Grand Central Madison. Located adjacent to Track 115. Includes new platform, direct fixation track, bridge, freight elevator, and Sonozaire odor neutralizer.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North and LIRR Unified Trash Facility Debuts at Grand Central

tool_args.label: Stamford Station Platform Elevator and Escalator Replacement

payload.project_name: Stamford Station Platform Elevator and Escalator Replacement
payload.status: Track 2 platform outage expected completed August 2023; overall project completion anticipated May 2024
payload.description: Connecticut DOT project to replace platform elevators and escalators at Stamford station, Metro-North's busiest station outside Grand Central Terminal. Contractor to replace elevators on each platform one at a time.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Stamford Station Work & Metro-North Schedule Changes

payload.description: Restoration of the Track 3 Beacon Station platform on the Upper Hudson Line. Includes restored platform surface and new ADA-compliant ramp. Allows two trains to pass each other instead of single-track operation during maintenance work. Also used to turn around Beacon Bullet express trains and Leaf Peeper trains.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Beacon Track 3 Work Progresses

tool_args.label: Long Island City Yard Flood Wall

payload.project_name: Long Island City Yard Flood Wall
payload.status: expect beneficial use in spring of 2024
payload.description: Constructing a perimeter flood wall with 9 flood gates, 3 pumping stations with backup generators, and a new electrified pair of tracks for equipment cleaning at Long Island City Yard. Also includes utility pole relocation and yard lighting restoration.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Sandy-Inspired Strengthening in Long Island City

payload.description: Federal government committing $292 million towards a critical early phase of the Hudson Tunnel Project that will improve reliability on Amtrak's Northeast Corridor. One of nine projects nationwide helped as part of the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law's discretionary grant program.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Hail to the Chief Along the West Side

payload.description: Replacing the high-level platform and implementing other improvements at Northport Station on the Port Jefferson Branch. New 12-car high-level concrete platform, ADA ramp, aluminum railings, platform shelters, boiler house, and Automatic Snow and Ice Melting System.

extra_fields.location: Northport Station, Port Jefferson Branch, 40 miles east of Penn Station

payload.status: approximately 25% complete; expected substantial completion in third quarter of 2023

evidence_refs.source_quote: Northport Station and Platform is New and Improved

payload.description: $16.5 million in safety improvements at ten LIRR highway-rail grade crossings. Improvements include safety upgrades to crossings, traffic signal upgrades, railroad flashing-light signals, audible warning devices, enhanced pedestrian treatments, signage, pavement markings, roadway resurfacing, sidewalk expansion, and vegetation management.

extra_fields.partner_agency: New York State Department of Transportation

payload.status: anticipated to commence Spring 2023 and be completed in 2027

evidence_refs.source_quote: LIRR Awarded $16.5 Million for Grade Crossing Safety Projects

payload.description: $26.3 million in funding for safety improvements at 14 grade crossings along the Hudson and Harlem Lines. Crossings in six towns in Dutchess, Putnam, and Westchester Counties.

extra_fields.partner_agency: New York State Department of Transportation

payload.status: anticipated to commence Spring 2023 and be completed in 2027

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North Awarded $26 Million for Grade Crossing Safety Projects

payload.description: Grand opening of Grand Central Madison, the new LIRR terminal at Grand Central

evidence_refs.source_quote: beginning with the grand opening on January 25

tool_args.label: Grand Central Madison Full Service Begins

payload.description: Full service to Grand Central Madison begins with Monday, February 27 timetable change. Rush hour service split between Penn Station and Grand Central Madison.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Full service to Grand Central Madison begins with our Monday, February 27 timetable change

payload.description: President Joe Biden visited the LIRR's West Side Yard maintenance shop to announce $292 million federal commitment towards the Hudson Tunnel Project
participants: MTA Chair and CEO Janno Lieber

evidence_refs.source_quote: Hail to the Chief Along the West Side
